report_name: KillSec Ransomware Group Targets Nigerian Oil and Gas Firm MRS Holdings

threat_actors.name: KillSec
threat_actors.description: A Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) group that conducted a double-extortion attack against MRS Holdings, a Nigerian oil and gas company. The group listed the victim on its data leak site and threatened to publish stolen data if its ransom demands were not met.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The KillSec group likely used credentials previously stolen by infostealer malware to gain initial access to the MRS Holdings network, possibly through a VPN, RDP, or other remote service.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely exfiltrated large amounts of sensitive data to an external cloud storage service before deploying the ransomware to use as leverage for extortion.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The KillSec ransomware payload was deployed across the MRS Holdings network to encrypt servers and workstations, disrupting operations.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The KillSec ransomware likely used vssadmin to delete volume shadow copies, preventing or hindering system restoration from local backups.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping
attack_patterns.description: Infostealer malware collected employee and user credentials associated with MRS Holdings prior to the ransomware attack, which were then likely used for initial access.

malware.name: KillSec Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload deployed by the KillSec group to encrypt servers and workstations at MRS Holdings as part of a double-extortion attack.

malware.name: Infostealer
malware.description: Generic infostealer malware responsible for the initial compromise of at least 33 employee and user credentials associated with MRS Holdings. These stolen credentials were later used by the KillSec group for initial access.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: Likely used by the KillSec ransomware to delete volume shadow copies to inhibit system recovery.

identities.name: MRS Holdings
identities.description: A major Nigerian oil and gas company targeted by the KillSec ransomware group in a double-extortion attack.

infrastructure.name: KillSec Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the KillSec group to list MRS Holdings as a victim and threaten to publish stolen data to pressure the company into paying a ransom.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: c1d5b8e9-0f4a-4b1e-8b1c-9a2d3e4f5a6b
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://dexpose.com/killsec-ransomware-attack-on-mrs-holdings/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-10
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity, although rare.
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the command-line pattern for deleting volume shadow copies using vssadmin.exe, a behavior associated with ransomware attacks to hinder recovery.

indicators.name: Anomalous Large Data Egress to Cloud Storage
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Large Data Egress to Cloud Storage
id: d2e6c9f0-1a5b-5c2f-9c2d-0a3e4f5b6c7d
status: experimental
description: Detects potentially anomalous data transfers to known public cloud storage providers, which could indicate pre-ransomware data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://dexpose.com/killsec-ransomware-attack-on-mrs-holdings/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-10
tags:
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.exfiltration
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate large data backups or transfers to cloud services. This rule requires tuning based on traffic volume baselines.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-host|contains:
      - 'mega.nz'
      - 'dropbox.com'
      - 'drive.google.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Provides a hunting hint for detecting large data uploads to common cloud storage providers, a potential sign of data exfiltration before a ransomware attack.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous Large Data Egress to Cloud Storage
